description_long: This object is a modern urban traffic light system featuring a horizontal arrangement of three signal lights—red, yellow, and green—mounted on a sturdy black pole with a metallic finish. The lights are housed in square casings and are attached to a horizontal arm that extends over the roadway, supported by a vertical pole with a yellow safety marking at the base. Adjacent to the pole is a tall, rectangular control or payment terminal with a digital display, keypad, and a green indicator on top, suggesting it may serve as a parking meter or ticketing machine. The pole also features a small rectangular sign and a red tag, likely for identification or maintenance purposes. The materials appear to be primarily metal and plastic, with glass or polycarbonate lenses covering the signal lights. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for busy intersections or urban streets.
description: A horizontally mounted traffic light system with three colored signal lights on a black pole, accompanied by a tall control or payment terminal with a digital interface.
description_view_0: The traffic light is seen from the side, showing the slim profile of the pole and the edge of the signal lights, with the control terminal partially visible.
description_view_1: The front view displays all three signal lights—red, yellow, and green—clearly, as well as the full face of the control terminal with its digital display and keypad.
description_view_2: The opposite side view highlights the back of the signal lights and the pole, with the control terminal visible from the side.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the backs of the signal lights and the control terminal, with less detail on the lights themselves.
